case_brief: Patricia Okafor, a 45-year-old registered nurse at St. Augustine Hospital, was terminated for patient safety violations. On three separate occasions over 6 months, Okafor administered medication doses outside the physician-ordered range without documenting a clinical rationale. The incidents were flagged by the pharmacy system, reviewed by the Chief Nursing Officer, and Okafor received written warnings after the first two incidents with clear notice that a third violation would result in termination. After the third incident, she was terminated following a peer review committee recommendation. Okafor claims the termination was racially motivated, noting that a white nurse had a documentation error the prior year and was not terminated. However, the comparison nurse's error was a documentation omission, not an out-of-range dosing — a materially different infraction. Okafor had filed no prior discrimination complaints. Her employment is governed by a collective bargaining agreement requiring 'just cause' for termination. Jurisdiction: 6th Circuit (Ohio).
